closureBasis: Retained old-world families are admissible for fifth-gate only when their live-path role is explicitly classified as governance, ingress-only, compatibility-only, reference-only, or auxiliary-input rather than surviving as parallel product ownership.

checks.label: System-reform governance remains explicit in the active V26 family
checks.livePathRole: The canonical spec family and supplementary architecture/proof docs classify retained old-world families rather than leaving them implicit.

checks.label: Retained webhook carriers are explicit ingress-only automation boundaries
checks.livePathRole: GitHub-triggered automation ingress may schedule work, but it does not own Bitcode Exchange state semantics.

checks.label: Deliverable pipeline run export remains explicit compatibility-only carry-through
checks.livePathRole: Legacy path exports stay available for admitted callers while canonical Bitcode behavior remains elsewhere.

checks.label: Retained VCS agent layer remains explicit reference-only automation
checks.livePathRole: The VCS agent layer can support retained callers, but repository scope in the Bitcode product belongs to Connects and application-owned panels.

checks.label: Retained shell-execution tooling remains explicit reference-only automation
checks.livePathRole: Shell execution may support admitted tooling paths, but it is not a Bitcode state owner.

checks.label: Retained Jira tooling remains explicit reference-only ingress/integration support
checks.livePathRole: Jira tooling can contribute admitted integration context without becoming Bitcode Exchange ownership.

checks.label: Retained search carriers remain explicit auxiliary-input providers
checks.livePathRole: External search/research context may enrich admitted interfaces without becoming Bitcode Terminal or Exchange truth.
